history.qwen_observation: Spring Boot 2.x tests fail on JDK 17+ due to CGLIB InaccessibleObjectException when trying to access java.lang.ClassLoader.defineClass; the project needs Spring Boot 3.x which requires Java 17+ and uses jakarta.* namespace
history.qwen_rationale: Replace the upgrade_build_to_java17 step with a two-step Spring Boot migration: first upgrade to SB 2.7 under JDK 11 (which supports Java 11 and avoids the CGLIB module issue), then upgrade to SB 3.3 under JDK 17 (which handles javax→jakarta migration and JDK 17+ requirement). The SB-3 meta-recipe internally composes the JDK upgrade, so we can then continue with Java 17 and Java 21 transforms.

history.qwen_observation: POM parsing failed during Spring Boot 2.7 upgrade due to missing versions for com.jcraft:jsch and deprecated Spring Cloud Netflix starters (ribbon, eureka, hystrix, turbine, zuul) that are no longer managed by the newer Spring Cloud BOM.
history.qwen_rationale: Insert a step before the SB 2.7 upgrade to explicitly version jsch and remove the deprecated Spring Cloud Netflix dependencies that were dropped in Spring Cloud 2020.0.0. This resolves the Maven POM validation errors and allows the Spring Boot upgrade to proceed without BOM management conflicts.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed at upgrade_spring_boot_to_2_7 because the Spring Boot 2.7 upgrade altered dependency management for com.jcraft:jsch, leaving it without a version tag in multiple modules.
history.qwen_rationale: Moving the jsch version fix to immediately after the Spring Boot 2.7 upgrade ensures the version is explicitly set after the BOM changes. Deprecated Netflix dependencies are removed beforehand to prevent conflicts with newer Spring Cloud BOMs. The rest of the chain proceeds with standard JDK and Spring Boot upgrades.

history.qwen_observation: The upgrade_spring_boot_to_2_7 step fails post-recipe because Spring Boot 2.7's dependency management no longer provides a version for com.jcraft:jsch, causing a Maven POM validation error during the compile phase.
history.qwen_rationale: By inserting a dedicated step to explicitly version the jsch dependency before the Spring Boot 2.7 upgrade, we ensure the POM remains valid after the BOM changes take effect, allowing the build to proceed to the Spring Boot 3.x and Java 21 migrations.
